query: VHS scanlines TV static analog distortion CRT screen
message: sub:analog_degradation/VHS_CRT — anti_aesthetic — scanlines, TV static, color bars, pixel grids, retro CRT

response.objective_caption: A close-up view of a cracked, reflective surface, possibly shattered glass or plastic, with light creating rainbow-colored streaks along the fracture lines. The surface is broken into irregular angular fragments, with bright illumination concentrated in the upper left corner. The image is framed by a solid green border.
response.anti_aesthetic_caption: A digitally degraded close-up of a cracked reflective surface, possibly shattered glass or plastic, overwhelmed by aggressive digital artifacts, blocky compression, and severe chromatic aberration that pulls colors apart at every edge. Toxic neon greens, reds, yellows, and magentas clash in violent disharmony across the frame, while the rainbow refractions along the fracture lines dissolve into pixelated abstraction. The subject is rendered nearly unrecognizable beneath layers of color fringing and formal distortion, suffused with an aggressive, unsettling palette and enclosed by a harsh green border.
